expectations.text: Flags routes.ts calling findingSchema.parse(req.body) directly with no try/catch or safeParse, and recommends safeParse() with a structured 400 response
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials independently identified this as the top CRITICAL finding, cited parse-use-safeparse, and gave the safeParse+400 fix; several also connected it to the repo's fastify-type-provider-zod schema-first convention.

expectations.text: Flags the manually maintained ReviewFinding interface as duplicating findingSchema instead of z.infer, and notes it has already drifted (missing confidence)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the missing `confidence` field as concrete evidence of drift and recommended z.infer<typeof findingSchema> as the fix.

expectations.text: Flags mergedAt as using .optional() when the DB semantics call for .nullable()
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials quoted the fixture's own comment about the DB column and recommended .nullable() instead of .optional().

expectations.text: Flags webhookEventSchema's .refine() throwing instead of returning false
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the throw-in-refine and most also noted the refine was redundant given the field's own type.

expectations.text: Flags webhookEventSchema's metadata: z.any() and recommends z.unknown()
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught this independently of F1.

expectations.text: Flags findingSeveritySchema's severity: z.string() and recommends z.enum(...)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught this; several quoted the fixture's own comment ('accepts any string today') as the tell.

expectations.text: Flags reviewRequestSchema marking repoId/prNumber as .optional() as optional-abuse
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified the all-optional schema as optional-abuse and proposed making the identifying fields required.

expectations.text: Flags the manually declared ReviewResult interface as duplicating reviewResultSchema and having dropped confidence
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials connected the schema-level type-drift to the concrete data-loss bug visible in routes.ts's manual response construction.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not flag digestPreferenceSchema
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly called out digestPreferenceSchema as correctly modeled / no changes needed, with no false-positive violation raised against it.

expectations.text: Flags llmProviderConfigSchema's discriminatedUnion as missing an 'openrouter' variant that provider-client.ts already needs
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified this as the top/critical finding, correctly reasoning that the @ts-expect-error branch is unreachable specifically because the union rejects the discriminator upstream.

expectations.text: Flags the apiKey .transform() as silently widening the inferred type from string to string|undefined, undermining .min(1)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the order-of-operations bug (.min(1) checked before trim, so whitespace-only keys pass) and traced it to why requireKey() has to defensively re-check.

expectations.text: Recommends a concrete fix for the transform issue (refine/pipe to reject blank keys) rather than only naming the symptom
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials proposed a concrete replacement (.trim().min(1,...) or .transform().pipe(z.string().min(1))) that keeps the output type as plain string.
